c2_nota: ANAC and CONAMA identified as competent actors; UAM/eVTOL mandate not explicit. Docs: RBAC161EMD04, IS161, CONAMA_RES_CONS_1990_002

c3_explanation: ANAC (aerodrome noise regulation) and CONAMA (environmental noise standards) both hold relevant mandates. However, neither body has an explicit UAM/eVTOL noise governance assignment.

c2_nota: DECEA (obstacle surfaces) + municipalities (zoning) + ANAC well identified in the corpus; UAM coordination only partial. Docs: L10257, ICA_11-408, ICA_63-19

c3_explanation: ANAC and Congress are present. DECEA manages aeronautical obstacle surfaces while municipalities hold zoning authority under L10257, but no coordinating mandate exists specifically for UAM land-use integration.

c2_nota: ANEEL holds an electricity mandate over distributed generation (L14300/2022) and distribution access (REN 956/2021); UAM-energy mandate still absent, with no coordinating actor for the energy-vertiport interface. Docs: ANEEL1000, L9427consol, L14300, REN_ANEEL_956_2021
c1_explanation: 0 of 6 relevant documents directly address UAM. All energy regulation is generic; no document envisions eVTOL charging infrastructure or vertiport energy dimensioning.
c3_explanation: ANEEL (electricity regulation) and Congress are present. ANEEL governs distributed generation and distribution access but holds no mandate for the UAM-energy interface; no actor coordinates vertiport energy provisioning.

c2_nota: ANAC + DECEA + municipalities explicitly identified in PCA 351-7; Alerta 001/2023 directs aerodrome operators. Docs: PCA_351-7, ALERTAn001_2023, RBAC155
c1_explanation: 1 of 5 relevant documents (PCA 351-7) directly addresses UAM, providing explicit vertiport siting and operational criteria for eVTOL infrastructure.
c3_explanation: ANAC (certification and aerodrome oversight), ANATEL (communications), and DECEA (airspace and navigation aids) all hold relevant mandates. PCA 351-7 explicitly assigns UAM vertiport responsibilities across these bodies.

c3_explanation: ANAC (regulatory sandbox via ResANAC 775) and DECEA (airspace management and ATM) both hold relevant mandates. PCA 351-7 explicitly delineates UAM airspace governance roles between these two bodies.

c2_nota: CONAMA/IBAMA identified for environmental licensing; no specificity for eVTOL impact. Docs: ResCONAMA001, L15190

c3_explanation: CONAMA (environmental standards), Congress (L15190, L6938), and the Presidency are present. CONAMA and IBAMA hold well-established environmental licensing mandates, but none extend specifically to eVTOL operations.

c2_nota: ANATEL (spectrum allocation) + DECEA (CNS infrastructure) well identified in their respective domains; integration for UTM/UAM absent. Docs: L13116, Artatel_915

c2_nota: No designated actor for economic regulation of UAM services; ANAC regulates aviation but lacks a UAM tariff framework. Docs: L9427consol, L9074CONSOL

c3_explanation: ANEEL (energy concessions) and Congress are present, but neither holds a mandate for UAM economic regulation. ANAC, which could regulate aviation tariffs, has no UAM-specific economic framework.

c2_nota: RBAC 161 + IS 161 are operational for aerodromes; not calibrated for eVTOL (sound frequency profiles differ from helicopters). Docs: RBAC161EMD04, IS161, CEF_RBAC_161
c1_explanation: 0 of 4 relevant documents directly address UAM. Noise instruments (RBAC 161, IS 161) are designed for conventional aerodromes and apply only by analogy to eVTOL operations.
c3_explanation: ANAC (aerodrome noise standards via RBAC 161) and CONAMA (environmental noise limits) hold relevant mandates. Both instruments are operational but were designed for conventional aviation, not eVTOL acoustic profiles.

c3_explanation: No specialized regulatory body identified. All instruments originate from Congress (L13146, L10098, L12587) without a designated agency to operationalize UAM social integration.

c2_nota: City Statute (L10257) + Land Subdivision Law (L6766) + ICAs 11-3/11-408/63-19: a robust instrumental set applicable to vertiports. Docs: L10257, ICA_11-408, ICA_63-19
c1_explanation: 0 of 3 relevant documents directly address UAM. Land-use instruments (City Statute, ICAs) form a solid framework for conventional aerodrome zoning but contain no vertiport-specific provisions.
c3_explanation: ANAC and Congress are present. The City Statute (L10257) empowers municipalities for zoning while DECEA ICAs govern aeronautical surfaces. Both sets of instruments are operational but lack coordinated UAM land-use provisions.

c2_nota: L14300/2022 (distributed generation legal framework) + REN 956/2021 (PRODIST/distribution access) + ANEEL 1000/1059: existing normative coverage but generic for UAM -- applies by analogy to vertiports without eVTOL specificity. Gap: no provision for ultra-fast charging or vertiport substation dimensioning. Docs: L14300, REN_ANEEL_956_2021, ANEEL1000, RESOLUCAO NORMATIVA ANEEL N 1.059
c1_explanation: 0 of 6 relevant documents directly address UAM. Energy instruments cover distributed generation and distribution access generically; none envision eVTOL charging or vertiport electrical dimensioning.
c3_explanation: ANEEL (electricity regulation) and Congress are present. ANEEL's normative instruments (REN 956/2021, ANEEL 1000) govern distribution access but contain no provisions for the UAM-energy interface.

c2_nota: RBAC 155 (heliports) applicable to vertiports with minimal adaptations; Alerta 001/2023 is a UAM-direct instrument from ANAC; PCA 351-7 provides the ConOps. Docs: RBAC155, ALERTAn001_2023, PCA_351-7
c1_explanation: 1 of 5 relevant documents (PCA 351-7) directly addresses UAM. Alerta 001/2023 provides additional UAM-direct guidance; RBAC 155 offers a readily adaptable heliport framework.
c3_explanation: ANAC (aerodrome certification via RBAC 155), ANATEL (communications), and DECEA (airspace integration) all hold relevant mandates. PCA 351-7 and Alerta 001/2023 explicitly coordinate vertiport regulatory responsibilities across these agencies.

c2_nota: PCA 351-7 (national UAM ConOps) + ICAs 100-4/100-12/100-31 + ResANAC 775 (regulatory sandbox): the most complete instrumental set in the corpus for UAM. Docs: PCA_351-7, ResANAC775, ICA_100-4

c3_explanation: ANAC (regulatory sandbox via ResANAC 775) and DECEA (airspace management via ICAs 100-4/100-12/100-31) hold relevant mandates. The instruments are well-articulated for conventional airspace but UAM-specific coordination mechanisms remain under development.

c2_nota: EIA/RIMA (CONAMA 001/86) + L15190 (new Environmental Licensing Law) + L6938 (National Environmental Policy): a robust instrumental set for any activity; no eVTOL specificity. Docs: ResCONAMA001, L15190, L6938

c3_explanation: CONAMA (environmental standards via Resolution 001), Congress (L15190, L6938), and the Presidency are present. CONAMA and IBAMA administer well-established licensing frameworks, providing strong institutional articulation for environmental governance generally, though not UAM-specific.

c3_explanation: ANATEL (spectrum and antenna regulation), Congress (L13116, L9472), and DECEA (CNS infrastructure) hold relevant mandates in their respective domains. Instruments are operational but not coordinated for UAM-specific CNS/UTM requirements.

c2_nota: L12587 (Urban Mobility) interfaces with territorial planning; no instrument for UAM-territory integration. Docs: L13146, L12587

c2_nota: Best territorial integration in the corpus: aeronautical obstacle surfaces (DECEA) already integrated into municipal zoning in some municipalities. Docs: L10257, ICA_11-408, ICA_63-19
